manor_merchant.name: Dusty Merchant
manor_merchant.description: A hunched figure in a tattered cloak stands behind a makeshift counter of stacked crates. His eyes glint in the dim light as he beckons you closer.

text: Ah, a visitor! Don't get many of those around here anymore.
I've got wares if you've got time. Name's Percival, but everyone calls me Dusty.

choices.label: What do you have for sale?

text: Best potions this side of the wastes. Brewed 'em myself!
Here, take this one on the house. First customer in ages deserves a freebie.
Don't mention it. You look like you'll need it where you're headed.
This place used to be grand, you know. The Lord of the Manor was a good man.
Then something crept up from below. From the cellar. The shadows started moving on their own.
Everyone left. Except me. Someone's gotta keep shop.
Brave? Nah. Just stubborn. And maybe a little bit crazy.
The cellar... I wouldn't go down there unprepared.
There's a SHADE down there. Not like a normal shadow -- this thing is alive.
You'll want a decent weapon and some potions before you even think about it.

choices.label: I have the rusty key. Will that help?

text: You found the old key! That'll get you past the locked wine racks.
Be ready for a fight though. The Shade doesn't take kindly to visitors.

wilds_guide.name: Forest Guide
wilds_guide.description: A weathered woman in green leather sits on a mossy stump, whittling a stick. A hawk perches on her shoulder, watching you with sharp eyes.

text: Hm. Another adventurer stumbling through my forest.
I'm Wren. I know every path in these wilds. Ask your questions.

choices.label: How do I get to the mountain?
Is there anything hidden in this forest?

text: The mountain lies to the north, past the clearing.
But the troll up there... he's no joke. Crushed the last three adventurers like tin cans.
You'll want steel at minimum. And don't try to run -- he's faster than he looks.
Trolls are slow to turn. Get behind him and strike fast.
Also... I've heard fire makes them flinch. If you find a fire weapon, use it.

choices.label: Know any secrets of the forest?

text: Secrets? This forest is FULL of them.
There's a clearing to the north with old stones. If you look carefully at the south edge...
Well. Let's just say not every path is obvious.
The mushrooms. Follow the mushrooms. That's all I'll say.
Some things you have to discover for yourself.
Help you? Hm. Let me see what you're made of first.
You've got some experience under your belt. I can see it in your eyes.
Here. Take this map. I found it in the old ruins years ago.
It shows paths that aren't on any normal chart. Use it wisely.
Come back when you've got a few battles under your belt.
The wilds don't respect the inexperienced.

wastes_hermit.name: Hermit of the Wastes
wastes_hermit.description: An old man wrapped in sun-bleached rags sits cross-legged in the dust. His eyes are milky white but somehow he seems to see everything.

text: The dust speaks your name. I have been waiting.
I am what remains when all else is forgotten.

choices.label: Do you have anything to trade?
What do you know about the ruins?

text: Names are for those who still belong somewhere.
I lived in the village once. Before the emptying.
Now I sit and I listen. The land tells stories if you're patient enough.
The people left when the water dried up. But the water didn't dry up on its own.
Something in the ruins... it DRINKS. It drinks the life from the land.
The guardian. It was supposed to protect us. Instead it consumed everything.
The ruins are what's left of the First City. Built before memory.
At its heart sits the Guardian. It was made to protect the city forever.
But forever is a long time. The Guardian forgot what it was protecting and started destroying instead.
Ironic, isn't it? The protector became the destroyer.

choices.label: How do I defeat the Guardian?
I need healing before I go.

text: Defeat it? Bold. Very bold.
The Guardian is stone and magic. Blunt force alone won't work.
But... it was made by mortal hands. And mortal hands make mistakes.
Look for the cracks. Literally. Strike where the stone is weakest.
You carry wounds. Not just of the body.
But the body I can mend.
Hold still.
There. Whole again. At least on the outside.
Go carefully. The wastes do not forgive carelessness.

hidden_cat_friend.name: Sir Whiskers III
hidden_cat_friend.description: A rotund tabby cat wearing a tiny top hat and monocle lounges on a velvet cushion. It speaks in a refined accent despite being, you know, a cat.

text: Ah, a human! How delightfully pedestrian.
I am Sir Whiskers the Third, Knight of the Mushroom Realm and personal friend of the great Milo.
Yes, THAT Milo. Don't act so surprised.
Know him? We're BESTIES. We went to cat school together.
He lives in the hobbit hole to the east. Fancies himself a duelist.
Between you and me, he cheats. Uses the old 'distract with a laser pointer' trick.
But don't tell him I said that. He gets... bitey.
Tips? Oh my dear human, Milo is unbeatable. He IS the final boss.
But if you insist on trying... he has a weakness for belly rubs.
Not that it'll help in combat. But it's adorable.
You want HELPFUL? I'm a cat. We don't DO helpful.
Fine, fine. Here. Take this fish. It's lucky. Probably.
I found it under a mushroom and it hasn't stopped glowing since.
There you go. One glowing fish, free of charge.
Now if you'll excuse me, I have a nap to get back to. Being this charming is exhausting.
A talking HUMAN? Now THAT'S weird.
Where I come from, ALL cats talk. It's the dogs that are quiet. As it should be.
You like it? It's vintage. Pre-owned by a very tiny gentleman.
The monocle is prescription. I'm nearsighted.
Being a fashionable cat in a mushroom dimension isn't easy, but someone has to do it.
Finally! A human with TASTE!
You know what, I like you. You may pet me exactly once.
*purrs aggressively*
*PURRRRRRR*
Okay that's enough. I have a reputation to maintain.
Tell no one of this.
This? This is the Shroom Dimension. Or as I call it, home.
It exists between the cracks of reality. The mushrooms hold it together.
Nobody really knows who built it. Some say a kid with a big imagination.
Personally, I think it's always been here. Waiting for someone fun to show up.
That's the rumor. A kid who made a whole world in a computer.
But worlds have a way of becoming... more than what they were made to be.
Don't think about it too hard. You'll get a headache.
